## `struct list_head` value fields directly in `task_struct` (`include/linux/sched.h:743`–`1554`)

Struct body confirmed by brace-matching (743 → 1554). No unnamed nested `struct {`/`union {` blocks exist inside `task_struct` (checked directly), so every field below sits at the top level, none anonymous.

| # | Field | Line | Gating |
|---|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | `rcu_node_entry` | 847 | `#ifdef CONFIG_PREEMPT_RCU` |
| 2 | `rcu_tasks_holdout_list` | 856 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_RCU` |
| 3 | `trc_holdout_list` | 863 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| 4 | `trc_blkd_node` | 864 | `#ifdef CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU` |
| 5 | `tasks` | 870 | unconditional |
| 6 | `children` | 989 | unconditional |
| 7 | `sibling` | 990 | unconditional |
| 8 | `ptraced` | 999 | unconditional |
| 9 | `ptrace_entry` | 1000 | unconditional |
| 10 | `thread_group` | 1005 | unconditional |
| 11 | `thread_node` | 1006 | unconditional |
| 12 | `cg_list` | 1227 | `#ifdef CONFIG_CGROUPS` |
| 13 | `pi_state_list` | 1238 | `#ifdef CONFIG_FUTEX` |
| 14 | `perf_event_list` | 1246 | `#ifdef CONFIG_PERF_EVENTS` |

**Total: 14 fields** — 7 unconditional, 7 inside `#ifdef` (4 distinct configs: `CONFIG_PREEMPT_RCU`, `CONFIG_TASKS_RCU`, `CONFIG_TASKS_TRACE_RCU`, plus one field each under `CONFIG_CGROUPS`, `CONFIG_FUTEX`, `CONFIG_PERF_EVENTS`). No fields fell inside an unnamed struct/union — that category is empty. All 14 are plain `struct list_head name;` (not `struct list_head *name`); pointer-typed list-head-ish fields (`robust_list`, etc.) were excluded by design.

Cross-check: `grep -c "struct list_head" ` over the same line range returned exactly 14 matches, matching the manual read.

## Real iteration example

```
kernel/exit.c:471:    list_for_each_entry(c, &p->children, sibling) {
```
Iterates `p->children` (field #6) using each child's own `sibling` field (#7) as the link — the standard parent→children walk in `find_new_reaper()`/mm-owner reassignment path.

(Ccodegraph itself doesn't model struct fields — only functions/globals/macros/files — so this task was done via targeted grep/awk over the header, cross-checked with a brace-matched scan of the full struct body plus a count sanity check.)
